title: Rich Smoky Uruguayan Beef Stew
description: A smoky and velvety stew featuring garlic powder and confit tomato, this dish is a romantic representation of Uruguayan cuisine, and is buddhist temple friendly as it does not contain any animal by products other than the beef itself.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium high heat.
Add the beef to the pot and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and red bell pepper to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the confit tomato and beef broth to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
